Aeollanthus neglectus, a plant species within the family Lamiaceae, is a flowering plant that has been traditionally used in herbal medicine. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help in the treatment of respiratory issues, wounds, and skin infections. Bioactive constituents of Aeollanthus neglectus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which may contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Aeollanthus neglectus is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for wound healing and skin care.
